sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Valladolid are in poor form, having lost their last 10 matches and failing to win in 17, with no clean sheets in that span. Deportivo Alavés have shown mixed results, with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss in their last 5 matches, indicating a more stable performance.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Real Valladolid won the most recent encounter 3-2 away, while Alavés secured a 1-0 home victory in the earlier matchup. Matches between these teams tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 4 of the last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Valladolid struggle offensively and defensively, ranking 20th in goals scored (26) and conceded (86). Alavés are slightly better, with 36 goals scored and 47 conceded, and they show a stronger late-game scoring trend (29.8% of goals in the 76-90 minute range).

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Valladolid, Raúl Moro is in form with a 6.92 average rating and recent contributions (1 goal, 3 assists). Alavés’ Nahuel Tenaglia stands out with a 7.6 rating over the last 5 matches, providing defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Valladolid’s high rate of conceding (no clean sheet in 17 games) could be exploited by Alavés’ ability to score late. However, Alavés’ games often see under 2.5 goals (8/8 recently), suggesting a tight contest.
